Dagger of Dropping  
 

Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY SECONDARY
Skill: 1H Piercing Atk Delay: 22
DMG: 4 Dmg Bonus: 35
Backstab DMG: 4
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
Effect: Weaken (Combat, Casting Time: Instant) at Level 15
WT: 1.5 Size: TINY
Class: ROG NEC WIZ MAG ENC BST
Race: ALL except HUM ERU HEF DRK
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

Kael Drakkel?
QuoteReply
# Aug 12 2003 at 11:26 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591770090910
18 posts
I had this drop off one of the Sergeants in Kael Drakkel today. He also dropped a Long Sword and a Beserker's Sword. He was using a sword and the dagger on me during the fight.
▲ Page top
RE: Kael Drakkel?
QuoteReply
# Jun 09 2004 at 6:34 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592260127882
111 posts
It was probably given to him as a weapon by an enchanter.
▲ Page top
damn gnolls
QuoteReply
# Mar 19 2002 at 5:02 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591773736565
5 posts
Funny I was soloing gnolls in splitpaw and one had this on me and it kept proc weaken and kicking my ***! Of course when I got it and decided to work a little on my piercing skill it never procs. Gonna give it to a necro pet I guess.
▲ Page top
Stooge #4
QuoteReply
# Feb 09 2002 at 10:23 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592251924332
Scholar
303 posts
I bought this bugger for my inky necro on an impulse, since I just happened to have the change on me. I figured what the hey, all my spells were already purchased so let's try it. Without the proc, this weapon is still not noticably better than any other dagger I've used. On top of that, I paid like 32 platinum and the resale value is strangely under 1 platinum. I could have gotten a Drachnid Leg Tip for 41 platinum and a better damage ratio. I forget how many shmucks tried to replace Curly, but I'll just say I felt like the Fourth Stooge.

Edited, Sat Feb 9 09:24:34 2002
▲ Page top
Beastlord
QuoteReply
# Nov 18 2001 at 12:49 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591761195679
203 posts
This weapon is usable by Beastlords (they somewhat specialize in piercing). My question is, does this imply that one of the listed races can be Beastlord? I don't recall seeing, for example, any weapons usable by ranger that aren't usable by a race that can be ranger, etc, even though the other way around is often broken (e.g. something rogue only usable by all non-large races). I'm hoping for a halfling beastlord when SOL comes out.
▲ Page top
RE: Beastlord
QuoteReply
# Apr 02 2002 at 1:37 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1631492832053
18 posts
My lvl 14 Beastlord is curently using one of these.

Give it to pets, ye give it to a lvl 15+ necro pet so its says "hi hi hi hi!" sounds kewl, do it!

Pet Weapon
QuoteReply
# Jul 23 2001 at 3:04 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592245869660
13 posts
I tend to stock up a whole bunch of these when I take my 37th warrior by the lair, He really has no use for them but I was wondering if giving them to my lower level sk would help, and could the pet make them proc?

Barnebus Goodfight
Commander of the Clan McMarrin
Brell server
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: May 06 2001 at 11:07 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) someone actually attempt to sell this thing for 100pp in Innoruuk's WC. He became laughing stock of the zone. Actually he tried to sell Split paw gloves for 200pp also.
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 11 2001 at 4:09 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) I bought this for 18pp but I found out iksars can't use em, very very gay.
Good Newbie Caster Item
QuoteReply
# Feb 24 2001 at 7:49 AM Rating: Decent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
As a newbie Necro, this dagger was the coolest. It was magical and it did ok damage. I used it up to the point that i could afford a dragoon dirk. I would suggest giving it to some (un-twinked)newbie as a gift, they'll thank you for it.
▲ Page top
HALF ELFs
QuoteReply
# Jan 13 2001 at 4:15 PM Rating: Decent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I have the item now and I can not use it. I am an agnostic HEF ROG.

I check the weapons stats and everything on this page is true except HEF
▲ Page top
RE: HALF ELFs
QuoteReply
# Jan 25 2001 at 12:23 AM Rating: Decent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
It looks to be a small-race only item. All races listed can wear small armor (some can wear medium, too), so that seems to be a common denominator.

Unless the race description has changed since you posted this, there is no HEF listed.
▲ Page top
Proc Level?
QuoteReply
# Jan 08 2001 at 7:00 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593965735719
17 posts
What level does this weapon proc?
▲ Page top
RE: Proc Level?
QuoteReply
# Mar 08 2002 at 5:56 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592251924332
Scholar
303 posts
Mine processes the effect at level 15. As a necro dark elf with decent deterity who still does melee alot, I can usually get it to fire off every battle or two. Combine this with the Vampiric Embrace spell and you've got a double proc. Its not great at 15, but its fun.
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Jan 25 2001 at 12:19 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Does it really matter? The "weakness"-series spells in PvE are useless anyway. The dmg/delay are nothing to write home about, either.
#REDACTED, Posted: Jul 11 2001 at 5:13 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Figures you'd be anonymous, Why do people who suck always end up being anonymous, It is a relevant question and if you can't help, why waste our time.
RE: Proc Level?
QuoteReply
# Jan 21 2001 at 3:29 AM Rating: Decent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Procs at 15.

Humans Can't use this
QuoteReply
# Dec 18 2000 at 9:04 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I just got one and Human Rogues can't use this.

I did get a cool weapon called a Crystalized Shadow Spear. Have not seen one any info about it but a great rogue weapon. I am just level 11 so this is great. Lots of plus points. I will add this weapon here later after work.

# Oct 29 2001 at 10:34 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592344474330

3,705 posts
Take that shadow spear to Stonebrunt mountains, and do the quest spirit aid, listed on this site. I think you will be very happy with the weapon you end upo with. +5 to all saves.
